steps.title: Fit line k from the graph
steps.content: Use a Desmos table for the line that is actually shown. Line $k$ passes through $(0,4)$ and $(3,0)$. A linear regression on those two points gives slope $-\frac{4}{3}$.

steps.title: Use the perpendicular slope
steps.content: Perpendicular lines have slopes that are negative reciprocals. Since line $k$ has slope $-\frac{4}{3}$, line $j$ has slope $\frac{3}{4}$.

steps.title: Use point-slope form with $(-20, -23)$
steps.content: $$y+23=\frac{3}{4}(x+20)$$ $$y=\frac{3}{4}x+15-23=\frac{3}{4}x-8$$ That matches choice A, so A is correct.
